mitre_det_name: Detection of Valid Account Abuse Across Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Detection of valid account misuse through SSH logins, sudo/su abuse, and service account anomalies outside expected patterns.

mitre_det_name: Detection of Valid Account Abuse Across Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Detection of valid account abuse in IdP logs via geographic anomalies, impossible travel, risky sign-ins, and multiple MFA attempts or failures.

mitre_det_name: Detection of Valid Account Abuse Across Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Detection of interactive and remote logins by service accounts or users at unusual times, with unexpected child process activity.

mitre_det_name: Detection of Valid Account Abuse Across Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Detection of containerized service accounts or compromised kubeconfigs being used for cluster access from unexpected nodes or IPs.

mitre_det_name: Detection of Valid Account Abuse Across Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Detection of compromised or misused valid accounts via anomalous logon patterns, abnormal logon types, and inconsistent geographic or time-based activity across Windows endpoints.

mitre_det_name: Detection of Multi-Platform File Encryption for Impact
mitre_an_desc: High-frequency file write operations using uncommon extensions, followed by ransom note creation, registry tampering, or shadow copy deletion. Often uses CLI tools like vssadmin, wbadmin, cipher, or PowerShell.

mitre_det_name: Detection of Multi-Platform File Encryption for Impact
mitre_an_desc: Ransomware encrypts .vmdk, .vmx, .log, or VM config files in VMFS datastores. May rename to .locked or delete/overwrite with encrypted versions. Often correlates with shell commands run through `dcui`, SSH, or vSphere.

mitre_det_name: Detection of Multi-Platform File Encryption for Impact
mitre_an_desc: Userland or kernel-level ransomware encrypting user files (Documents, Desktop) using `srm`, `gpg`, or compiled payloads. Often correlated with ransom note creation in multiple directories.

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: file encrypted|new file with .encrypted extension|disk write burst

mitre_det_name: Detection of Multi-Platform File Encryption for Impact
mitre_an_desc: Encryption of cloud storage objects (e.g., S3 buckets) via Server-Side Encryption (SSE-C) or by replacing objects with encrypted variants. May include API patterns like PutObject with SSE-C headers.

mitre_det_name: Detection of Multi-Platform File Encryption for Impact
mitre_an_desc: Encryption via custom or open-source tools (e.g., openssl, gpg, aescrypt) recursively targeting user or system directories. Also includes overwrite of existing data and ransom note drops.

mitre_det_name: Detection of Impair Defenses through Disabled or Modified Tools across OS Platforms.
mitre_an_desc: Detection of adversaries disabling cloud monitoring and logging agents such as CloudWatch, Google Cloud Monitoring, or Azure Monitor by API calls or agent process termination.

mitre_log_sources.name: AWS:CloudTrail
mitre_log_sources.channel: Delete* / Stop*: DeleteAlarms, StopLogging, or DisableMonitoring API calls

mitre_det_name: Detection of Impair Defenses through Disabled or Modified Tools across OS Platforms.
mitre_an_desc: Detection of adversary behavior that disables or modifies security tools, including killing AV/EDR processes, stopping services, altering Sysmon registry keys, or tampering with exclusion lists. Defenders observe process/service termination, registry modification, and abnormal absence of expected telemetry.

mitre_det_name: Detection of Impair Defenses through Disabled or Modified Tools across OS Platforms.
mitre_an_desc: Detection of adversary disabling endpoint security tools by unloading launch agents/daemons, modifying configuration profiles, or using security/uninstall commands to remove agents.

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: Execution of launchctl unload, kill, or removal of security agent daemons

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: Modification of system configuration profiles affecting security tools

mitre_det_name: Detection of Impair Defenses through Disabled or Modified Tools across OS Platforms.
mitre_an_desc: Detection of adversaries attempting to stop or disable host-based security agents by killing daemons, unloading kernel modules, or modifying init/systemd service configurations.

mitre_log_sources.name: auditd:SYSCALL
mitre_log_sources.channel: execve: systemctl stop, service stop, or kill -9 on security daemons (e.g., falcon-sensor, auditd)

mitre_log_sources.name: auditd:CONFIG_CHANGE
mitre_log_sources.channel: delete: Modification of systemd unit files or config for security agents

mitre_det_name: Detection of Impair Defenses through Disabled or Modified Tools across OS Platforms.
mitre_an_desc: Detection of adversaries modifying startup configuration files to disable signature verification, logging, or monitoring features.

mitre_log_sources.name: networkdevice:config
mitre_log_sources.channel: write: Startup configuration changes disabling security checks

mitre_det_name: Detection of Impair Defenses through Disabled or Modified Tools across OS Platforms.
mitre_an_desc: Detection of adversaries tampering with container runtime security plugins, disabling admission controllers, or stopping monitoring sidecars.

mitre_log_sources.name: kubernetes:audit
mitre_log_sources.channel: kubectl delete or patch of security pods/admission controllers

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection of Command and Scripting Interpreter Abuse
mitre_an_desc: Detects use of shell interpreters (e.g., bash, sh, python, perl) initiated by users or processes not normally executing them, especially when chaining suspicious utilities like netcat, curl, or ssh.

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection of Command and Scripting Interpreter Abuse
mitre_an_desc: Detects launch of command-line interpreters via Terminal, Automator, or hidden `osascript`, especially when parent process lineage deviates from user-initiated applications.

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection of Command and Scripting Interpreter Abuse
mitre_an_desc: Detects use of 'esxcli system' or direct interpreter commands (e.g., busybox shell) invoked from SSH or host terminal unexpectedly.

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection of Command and Scripting Interpreter Abuse
mitre_an_desc: Identifies CLI interpreter access (e.g., Cisco IOS, Juniper JUNOS) via `enable` mode or scripting-capable sessions used by uncommon accounts or from unknown IPs.

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection of Command and Scripting Interpreter Abuse
mitre_an_desc: Detects the execution of scripting or command interpreters (e.g., powershell.exe, cmd.exe, wscript.exe) outside expected administrative time windows or from abnormal user contexts, often followed by encoded/obfuscated arguments or secondary execution events.

mitre_det_name: Exploit Public-Facing Application – multi-signal correlation (request → error → post-exploit process/egress)
mitre_an_desc: Adversary exploits exposed OpenSLP on ESXi or vCenter public endpoints. Chain: inbound request pattern to mgmt service → hostd/vpxd error/crash/restart → unexpected process behavior or datastore access → outbound callback.

mitre_log_sources.name: esxi:hostd
mitre_log_sources.channel: /var/log/hostd.log anomalies (faults, crashes, restarts) around inbound connections

mitre_log_sources.name: NSM:Flow
mitre_log_sources.channel: Connections to TCP 427 (SLP) or vCenter web services from untrusted sources

mitre_det_name: Exploit Public-Facing Application – multi-signal correlation (request → error → post-exploit process/egress)
mitre_an_desc: Adversary sends crafted HTTP/S (or other service) input to an Internet-facing app (IIS/ASP.NET, API, device portal). Chain: (1) abnormal request patterns to public endpoint → (2) elevated 4xx/5xx or unusual methods/paths → (3) server process (w3wp.exe/other service) spawns shell/LOLbins or loads non-standard modules → (4) optional outbound callback from the host/container.

mitre_log_sources.name: ApplicationLog:IIS
mitre_log_sources.channel: IIS W3C logs in C:\inetpub\logs\LogFiles\W3SVC* (spikes in 5xx, RCE/SQLi/path traversal/JNDI patterns)

mitre_det_name: Exploit Public-Facing Application – multi-signal correlation (request → error → post-exploit process/egress)
mitre_an_desc: Adversary targets cloud-hosted public endpoints. Chain: (1) ALB/ELB/Cloud LB logs show exploit-like inputs or error spikes → (2) workload spawns shell or reaches metadata API → (3) egress to new external hosts.

mitre_log_sources.name: ALB:HTTPLogs
mitre_log_sources.channel: AWS ALB/ELB/GCP/Azure Application Gateway HTTP logs with unusual methods, long URIs, serialized payloads, 4xx/5xx bursts

mitre_log_sources.name: AWS:VPCFlowLogs
mitre_log_sources.channel: VPC/NSG flow logs for pod/instance egress to Internet or metadata

mitre_det_name: Exploit Public-Facing Application – multi-signal correlation (request → error → post-exploit process/egress)
mitre_an_desc: Adversary exploits public admin services on routers/firewalls/switches. Chain: anomalous HTTP/SNMP/SmartInstall inputs → device syslog errors/restarts → config changes/CLI spawn → egress to attacker C2.

mitre_log_sources.name: networkdevice:controlplane
mitre_log_sources.channel: Syslog from edge devices with HTTP 500s on mgmt portal, SmartInstall events, unexpected CLI commands

mitre_log_sources.name: NSM:Flow
mitre_log_sources.channel: NetFlow/sFlow for odd egress to Internet from mgmt plane

mitre_det_name: Exploit Public-Facing Application – multi-signal correlation (request → error → post-exploit process/egress)
mitre_an_desc: Adversary exploits Apache/Nginx/app servers. Chain: (1) suspicious requests in access logs → (2) spike of 5xx or WAF blocks → (3) web server or interpreter (apache2/nginx/php-fpm/node/python) spawns /bin/sh, curl, wget, socat, or writes webshell → (4) outbound callback.

mitre_log_sources.name: ApplicationLog:WebServer
mitre_log_sources.channel: /var/log/httpd/access_log, /var/log/apache2/access.log, /var/log/nginx/access.log with exploit indicators and burst errors

mitre_det_name: Exploit Public-Facing Application – multi-signal correlation (request → error → post-exploit process/egress)
mitre_an_desc: Adversary exploits containerized app via ingress or service. Chain: (1) suspicious request in ingress/app logs → (2) container process spawns a shell/exec/sidecar (kubectl exec/docker exec) → (3) egress to Internet or metadata service (169.254.169.254).

mitre_log_sources.name: ApplicationLog:Ingress
mitre_log_sources.channel: Kubernetes NGINX/Envoy ingress controller logs with anomalous payloads and 5xx spikes

mitre_log_sources.name: docker:events
mitre_log_sources.channel: Docker/Kubernetes audit of exec/attach (kubectl exec) or unexpected child processes inside container

mitre_log_sources.name: NSM:Flow
mitre_log_sources.channel: Requests towards cloud metadata or command & control from pod IPs

mitre_det_name: Exploit Public-Facing Application – multi-signal correlation (request → error → post-exploit process/egress)
mitre_an_desc: Adversary targets macOS-hosted public services (e.g., nginx, node). Chain: suspicious inbound request → service crash/5xx → service spawns shell or writes file → new outbound connection.

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: App/web server logs ingested via unified logging or filebeat (nginx/apache/node).

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: exec events where web process starts a shell/tooling

mitre_log_sources.name: NSM:Flow
mitre_log_sources.channel: outbound egress from web host after suspicious request

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection Strategy for Remote Service Logins and Post-Access Activity
mitre_an_desc: Logon via RDP or WMI by a user account followed by uncommon command execution, file manipulation, or lateral network connections.

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection Strategy for Remote Service Logins and Post-Access Activity
mitre_an_desc: vSphere API logins (vimService) or SSH to ESXi host followed by unauthorized shell commands or lateral remote logins from the ESXi host.

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection Strategy for Remote Service Logins and Post-Access Activity
mitre_an_desc: SSH session from new source IP followed by interactive shell or privilege escalation (e.g., sudo, su) and outbound lateral connection.

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection Strategy for Remote Service Logins and Post-Access Activity
mitre_an_desc: Use of cloud-based bastion or VM console session followed by commands that initiate outbound SSH or RDP sessions from the cloud instance to other environments.

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection Strategy for Remote Service Logins and Post-Access Activity
mitre_an_desc: Remote login via ARD or SSH followed by screensharingd process activity or modification of TCC-protected files.

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Phishing across platforms.
mitre_an_desc: Phishing attempts targeting IdPs often manifest as anomalous login attempts from suspicious email invitations or fake SSO prompts. Detection correlates login flows, MFA bypass attempts, and anomalous geographic patterns following phishing email delivery.

mitre_log_sources.name: azure:signinlogs
mitre_log_sources.channel: Failed MFA attempts, unusual conditional access triggers, login attempts from unexpected IP ranges

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Phishing across platforms.
mitre_an_desc: Detection of phishing through anomalous Mail app activity, such as attachments saved to disk and immediately executed, or Safari/Preview launching URLs and files linked from email messages. Correlate UnifiedLogs events with subsequent process execution.

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: Inbound email activity with suspicious domains or mismatched sender information

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: Preview.app, Safari.app, or Mail.app spawning new processes outside normal patterns

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Phishing across platforms.
mitre_an_desc: Phishing via Office documents containing embedded macros or links that spawn processes. Detection relies on correlating Office application logs with suspicious child process execution and outbound network connections.

mitre_log_sources.name: m365:unified
mitre_log_sources.channel: FileAccessed: Access of email attachments by Office applications

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Phishing across platforms.
mitre_an_desc: Phishing delivered via SaaS services (chat, collaboration platforms) where messages contain malicious URLs or attachments. Detect anomalous link clicks, suspicious file uploads, or token misuse after SaaS-based phishing attempts.

mitre_log_sources.name: saas:collaboration
mitre_log_sources.channel: MessagePosted: Suspicious links or attachment delivery via collaboration tools (Slack, Teams, Zoom)

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Phishing across platforms.
mitre_an_desc: Unusual inbound email activity where attachments or embedded URLs are delivered to users followed by execution of new processes or suspicious document behavior. Detection involves correlating email metadata, file creation, and network activity after a phishing message is received.

mitre_log_sources.name: m365:unified
mitre_log_sources.channel: Send/Receive: Emails with suspicious sender domains, spoofed headers, or anomalous attachment types

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Phishing across platforms.
mitre_an_desc: Monitor for malicious payload delivery through phishing where attachments or URLs in email clients (e.g., Thunderbird, mutt) result in unusual file creation or outbound network connections. Focus on correlation between mail logs, file writes, and execution activity.

mitre_log_sources.name: Application:Mail
mitre_log_sources.channel: Inbound messages with anomalous headers, spoofed SPF/DKIM failures

mitre_log_sources.name: auditd:SYSCALL
mitre_log_sources.channel: execve: Execution of scripts or binaries sourced from mail directories (/var/mail, ~/Maildir)

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Cloud Service Discovery
mitre_an_desc: Discovery of SaaS services connected to productivity platforms (e.g., Microsoft 365, Google Workspace). Defender perspective includes unexpected enumeration of enabled services, API integrations, or OAuth applications tied to user accounts.

mitre_log_sources.name: m365:unified
mitre_log_sources.channel: Get-MsolServicePrincipal, ListAppRoles: Service discovery operations executed by accounts not normally performing administrative tasks

mitre_log_sources.name: m365:signinlogs
mitre_log_sources.channel: UserLogin: Discovery operations shortly after account logins from new geolocations

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Cloud Service Discovery
mitre_an_desc: Discovery of connected SaaS applications, APIs, or configurations within platforms like Salesforce, Slack, or Zoom. Defender perspective includes enumeration of available integrations, abnormal querying of service metadata, and follow-on attempts to exploit or persist via discovered services.

mitre_log_sources.name: saas:adminapi
mitre_log_sources.channel: ListIntegrations, ListServices: Repeated service discovery requests from accounts without administrative responsibilities

mitre_log_sources.name: saas:auth
mitre_log_sources.channel: Login, TokenGranted: Discovery actions tied to anomalous login sessions or tokens

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Cloud Service Discovery
mitre_an_desc: Enumeration of directories, applications, or service principals through APIs such as Microsoft Graph or Okta API. Defender perspective includes unexpected listing of users, roles, applications, and abnormal access to identity management endpoints.

mitre_log_sources.name: azure:audit
mitre_log_sources.channel: ListApplications, ListServicePrincipals: Large-scale queries against identity or application objects

mitre_log_sources.name: azure:signinlogs
mitre_log_sources.channel: InteractiveUserLogin: Discovery behavior linked to privileged logins from atypical IP ranges

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Cloud Service Discovery
mitre_an_desc: Unusual enumeration of services and resources through cloud APIs such as AWS CLI `describe-*`, Azure Resource Manager queries, or GCP project listings. Defender perspective includes anomalous API calls, unexpected volume of service enumeration, and correlation of discovery with recently compromised sessions.

mitre_log_sources.name: AWS:CloudTrail
mitre_log_sources.channel: DescribeInstances, DescribeServices, ListFunctions: High frequency enumeration calls or unusual user agents performing discovery

mitre_log_sources.name: AWS:CloudTrail
mitre_log_sources.channel: AssumeRole: Discovery actions tied to assumed identities outside of normal context

mitre_det_name: Detection of Local Data Collection Prior to Exfiltration
mitre_an_desc: Adversaries accessing datastore or configuration files via `vim-cmd`, `esxcli`, or SCP to extract logs, VMs, or host configurations.

mitre_det_name: Detection of Local Data Collection Prior to Exfiltration
mitre_an_desc: Adversaries using bash scripts or tools to recursively enumerate user home directories, config files, or SSH keys.

mitre_det_name: Detection of Local Data Collection Prior to Exfiltration
mitre_an_desc: Adversaries collecting local files via PowerShell, WMI, or direct file API calls often include recursive file listings, targeted file reads, and temporary file staging.

mitre_det_name: Detection of Local Data Collection Prior to Exfiltration
mitre_an_desc: Adversary use of bash/zsh or AppleScript to locate files and exfil targets like user keychains or documents.

mitre_det_name: Detection of Local Data Collection Prior to Exfiltration
mitre_an_desc: Collection of device configuration via CLI commands (e.g., `show running-config`, `copy flash`, `more`), often followed by TFTP/SCP transfers.

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Impair Defenses Across Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Execution of commands that stop or kill processes associated with logging or security daemons (auditd, syslog, falco). Detect modifications to iptables or disabling SELinux/AppArmor enforcement. Correlate sudo/root context with abrupt service halts.

mitre_log_sources.name: auditd:EXECVE
mitre_log_sources.channel: systemctl stop auditd, kill -9 <pid>, or modifications to /etc/selinux/config

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Impair Defenses Across Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Changes to security configurations such as disabling MFA requirements, reducing session token lifetimes, or turning off risk-based policies. Correlate admin logins with sudden policy downgrades.

mitre_log_sources.name: azure:policy
mitre_log_sources.channel: DisableMfaPolicy or change to ConditionalAccess rules

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Impair Defenses Across Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Modification of container runtime security profiles (AppArmor, seccomp) or removal of monitoring agents within containers. Detect unauthorized mounting/unmounting of host /proc or /sys to disable logging or auditing.

mitre_log_sources.name: docker:runtime
mitre_log_sources.channel: Termination of monitoring sidecar or security container

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Impair Defenses Across Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Disabling of security macros or safe mode settings within Word/Excel/Outlook. Detect registry edits or configuration file changes that weaken macro enforcement.

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Impair Defenses Across Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Cloud control plane actions disabling security services (CloudTrail logging, GuardDuty, Security Hub). Detect IAM role abuse correlating with service disable events.

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Impair Defenses Across Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Unusual ESXi shell commands disabling syslog forwarding or stopping hostd/vpxa daemons. Detect modifications to firewall rules on ESXi host or disabling of lockdown mode.

mitre_log_sources.name: esxi:shell
mitre_log_sources.channel: esxcli system syslog config set --loghost='' or stopping hostd service

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Impair Defenses Across Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Execution of commands disabling AAA, logging, or security features on routers/switches. Detect privilege escalation followed by config changes that disable defense mechanisms.

mitre_log_sources.name: networkdevice:syslog
mitre_log_sources.channel: no logging buffered, no aaa new-model, disable firewall

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Impair Defenses Across Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Execution of commands or APIs that disable Gatekeeper, XProtect, or system integrity protections. Detect configuration changes through unified logs. Monitor termination of system security daemons (e.g., syspolicyd).

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: spctl --master-disable, csrutil disable, or defaults write to disable Gatekeeper

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: Termination of syspolicyd or XProtect processes

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Impair Defenses Across Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Unusual service stop events, termination of AV/EDR processes, registry modifications disabling security tools, and firewall/defender configuration changes. Correlate process creation with service stop requests and registry edits.

mitre_det_name: Detection of Web Session Cookie Theft via File, Memory, and Network Artifacts
mitre_an_desc: Detects unauthorized access to browser cookie paths (e.g., `~/Library/Application Support/Google/Chrome/Default/Cookies`) or `task_for_pid`/`vm_read` calls to Safari/Chrome memory space.

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: vm_read, task_for_pid, or file open to cookie databases

mitre_log_sources.name: fs:fsusage
mitre_log_sources.channel: file open for known browser cookie paths

mitre_det_name: Detection of Web Session Cookie Theft via File, Memory, and Network Artifacts
mitre_an_desc: Detects use of session cookies or authentication tokens from unusual user agents or locations. Identifies token reuse without reauthentication or attempts to bypass MFA using previously stolen cookies.

mitre_log_sources.name: saas:googleworkspace
mitre_log_sources.channel: login with reused session token and mismatched user agent or IP

mitre_det_name: Detection of Web Session Cookie Theft via File, Memory, and Network Artifacts
mitre_an_desc: Detects automation macros or VBA scripts in documents that access browser file paths, read cookie data, or attempt to exfiltrate browser session tokens over HTTP.

mitre_det_name: Detection of Web Session Cookie Theft via File, Memory, and Network Artifacts
mitre_an_desc: Detects access to known browser cookie files (e.g., `~/.mozilla/firefox/*.default/cookies.sqlite`, `~/.config/google-chrome/`) and suspicious reads of browser memory via `/proc/[pid]/mem` or ptrace.

mitre_log_sources.name: auditd:SYSCALL
mitre_log_sources.channel: open or read to browser cookie storage

mitre_det_name: Detection of Web Session Cookie Theft via File, Memory, and Network Artifacts
mitre_an_desc: Detects suspicious access to browser session cookie storage (e.g., Chrome’s `Cookies` SQLite DB) or memory reads of browser processes. Anomalous injection or memory dump utilities targeting browser processes such as `chrome.exe`, `firefox.exe`, or `msedge.exe`.

mitre_det_name: Multi-event Detection Strategy for RDP-Based Remote Logins and Post-Access Activity
mitre_an_desc: Remote Desktop (RDP) logon by a user followed by unusual process execution, file access, or lateral movement activity within a short timeframe.
